merchant_editorial: Come on a journey of amazing discovery through SEA LIFE Melbourne Aquarium. Discover a mesmerising range of marine and freshwater creatures including jellyfish, one of Australia’s largest saltwater crocodiles, hundreds of fish species and more! Along your journey, you’ll encounter some of the world’s most incredible animals and immerse yourself in an underwater paradise through state-of-the-art experiences in beautifully themed habitats. Journey beneath the surface of Melbourne’s iconic Port Phillip Bay to our Bay of Rays where you can gaze at the graceful Fiddler rays and other residents like the Port Jackson shark. Get your hands wet at the Interactive Discovery Rockpools and explore some of the extraordinary, squishy creatures in our Ocean Invaders exhibit. Relax on the steps of our window into the underwater world at the immersive Shipwreck Theatre and encounter some of the Aquarium’s largest marine creatures. Dive into Night on the Reef, an underwater wonderland showcasing the ocean’s natural bioluminescence. Come face-to-face with Pinjarra, one of Australia’s largest saltwater crocodiles in the Crocodile Lair before heading to the Penguin Playground where you can chill out and marvel at our mischievous king and gentoo penguins in action.

merchant_editorial:
Budj Bim Cultural Landscape Tourism offers guided cultural tours of southwestern Victoria’s deep time Gunditjmara Country. Inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage list for its outstanding universal values, the Budj Bim Cultural Landscape has ancient aquaculture systems which were first constructed at least 6,600 ago. The region is renowned for its age-old lava flows and life-filled waterways, and the remains of over 300 Aboriginal stone houses. 
Explore significant Gunditjmara sites on a guided tour and discover a vibrant Indigenous culture through shared stories and see the landscape through Gunditjmara eyes. Visit the Tae Rak Aquaculture Centre & Café where the menu features local produce infused with native seasonings, and the Gunditjmara chef introduces kooyang (eel) to diners in the most creative and delicious way.
